behaviors.description: The conversation history passed to the LLM is trimmed to the most recent 10 messages when the full history exceeds the context window.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is a cross-module handoff check (history manager -> prompt builder -> LLM client) from the phrase "passed to the LLM is trimmed to the most recent 10 messages." Unit tests on trim logic alone can miss wiring/order regressions. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: no explicit real OS/network/subprocess requirement, no dependence on model output quality, no multi-agentic chain. Rejection-gate: System rejected because behavior text does not require external contract/round-trip side effect; Agentic rejected because correctness is about deterministic payload shaping, not what the model decides.
behaviors.test_description: Run an in-process integration test across the chat service and LLM request adapter, inject >10-message history, and assert the outgoing LLM payload contains exactly the latest 10 messages in correct order.

behaviors.description: The generated response addresses the customer's question rather than producing a generic or off-topic reply.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: correctness is explicitly response quality/relevance ("addresses the customer's question" vs "generic or off-topic"), which a stub cannot validate. MOCK-HIDE: mocked outputs would always pass chosen fixtures and hide real model relevance failures. Rejection-gate: Workflow rejected because behavior text describes a single generation quality check, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Execute a real-model evaluation on a labeled prompt set and score whether responses stay on-topic and answer intent; fail if relevance metrics/acceptance thresholds are not met.

behaviors.description: The tone re-check correctly identifies when a response is too formal for a casual-tier customer and triggers regeneration.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: "correctly identifies when a response is too formal" is model judgment quality, so real model behavior is required. The trigger path is orchestration around that judgment. Rejection-gate: Workflow considered, but disqualified by behavior text focusing on one governing model decision (tone identification) that causes regeneration; it does not require validating multiple sequential model decisions for correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Use real LLM/tone-check calls with casual-tier scenarios and assert that overly formal drafts are flagged and a regeneration attempt is invoked by orchestration code.

behaviors.description: The full exchange (user message, generated response, any regenerations) is persisted to the database with correct timestamps.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is module interaction/state propagation (chat pipeline -> persistence layer), including multiple records and timestamp fields. A pure unit test can miss transaction/serialization mapping bugs. REAL-INFRA: behavior names database persistence but does not explicitly require external API/OS/subprocess contract checks; local DB integration is sufficient. Rejection-gate: System rejected because no explicit SYSTEM TRIAD external contract requirement is stated beyond local persistence semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Run integration test with a real local test DB, execute a flow including regeneration, then read back persisted rows and verify message sequence, inclusion of regenerated attempts, and timestamp correctness/ordering.

behaviors.description: When the tone check triggers regeneration, the second response replaces the first — the customer never sees the rejected draft.
behaviors.justification: SYSTEM TRIAD #3: behavior explicitly requires externally visible side-effect proof ("customer never sees the rejected draft"). This needs round-trip observation at the external boundary (e.g., real HTTP/API response), not just in-process return values. Rejection-gate: Agentic rejected because the behavior conditions on "when the tone check triggers" and primarily verifies exposure/replacement semantics, not model output quality itself.
behaviors.test_description: Exercise the deployed chat entrypoint over real HTTP, force/regulate a regeneration path, and assert the customer-facing response contains only the accepted second draft while internal logs may retain the rejected one.
